sourceMovie.title: Rock of Ages
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Rock of Ages (2012) stuck because it commits to its comedy premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Adam Shankman's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The comedy and drama blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: We Are Your Friends
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Rock of Ages clicked for you, We Are Your Friends (2015) is a strong next stop: both trade in comedy and drama, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Rock of Ages?
faq.answer: Try We Are Your Friends, The High Note, Café Society—each shares DNA with Rock of Ages while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Rock of Ages?
faq.answer: Rock of Ages (2012) is best described as Comedy, Drama, Romance, Music. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Rock of Ages worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.2/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this comedy lane.
